koko-eating-bananas.name: Brute Force
koko-eating-bananas.description: Try every integer eating speed from one upward and return the first speed that fits the hour limit.

implement-lower-bound.name: Brute Force
implement-lower-bound.description: Scan from the left and stop at the first value that is not smaller than the target.

implement-upper-bound.name: Brute Force
implement-upper-bound.description: Scan from the left and stop at the first value strictly greater than the target.

search-insert-position.name: Brute Force
search-insert-position.description: Linearly locate the first element not smaller than the target; its index is the insertion position.

floor-ceil-in-sorted-array.name: Brute Force
floor-ceil-in-sorted-array.description: Inspect every value and retain the greatest value below the target and the smallest value above it.

first-or-last-occurrence-of-a-number.name: Brute Force
first-or-last-occurrence-of-a-number.description: Visit every position and update the first and last indices whenever the target appears.

minimum-days-to-make-m-bouquets.name: Brute Force
minimum-days-to-make-m-bouquets.description: Test every calendar day between the earliest and latest bloom and simulate bouquet construction.

find-the-smallest-divisor-given-a-thresh.name: Brute Force
find-the-smallest-divisor-given-a-thresh.description: Try divisors in increasing order and directly compute the rounded-up division sum.

capacity-to-ship-packages-within-d-days.name: Brute Force
capacity-to-ship-packages-within-d-days.description: Enumerate every feasible capacity and simulate the number of shipping days it requires.

book-allocation-problem.name: Brute Force
book-allocation-problem.description: Try every possible maximum page limit and greedily count how many students that limit needs.

find-the-row-with-maximum-number-of-1s.name: Brute Force
find-the-row-with-maximum-number-of-1s.description: Count the ones in every row and retain the row with the largest count.

search-in-a-2d-matrix-ii.name: Brute Force
search-in-a-2d-matrix-ii.description: Inspect every matrix cell until the target is found.

find-peak-element-2d-matrix.name: Brute Force
find-peak-element-2d-matrix.description: Check every cell against its four neighbors and return the first local peak.

kth-missing-positive-number.name: Brute Force
kth-missing-positive-number.description: Walk through positive integers and count values absent from the sorted input until the kth miss.

subarray-sum-equals-k.name: Brute Force
subarray-sum-equals-k.description: Enumerate every starting point, extend the subarray, and count each running sum equal to k.

rotate-matrix-by-90-degrees.name: Brute Force
rotate-matrix-by-90-degrees.description: Write each source cell into its rotated location in a second matrix.

left-rotate-an-array-by-d-places.name: Brute Force
left-rotate-an-array-by-d-places.description: Perform a one-position left shift exactly d times.

second-largest-element-in-an-array-witho.name: Brute Force
second-largest-element-in-an-array-witho.description: Sort a copy, skip duplicate maxima, and return the next distinct value.

counting-frequencies-of-array-elements.name: Brute Force
counting-frequencies-of-array-elements.description: For each unprocessed value, scan the full array to count its occurrences.

find-the-highest-lowest-frequency-elemen.name: Brute Force
find-the-highest-lowest-frequency-elemen.description: Count every candidate with a nested scan and track the values with minimum and maximum frequency.

find-missing-number-in-an-array.name: Brute Force
find-missing-number-in-an-array.description: Try every value in the expected range and linearly search for it in the input.

find-the-union.name: Better
find-the-union.description: Insert both arrays into an ordered set to deduplicate and sort their union.

longest-subarray-with-given-sum-k-positi.name: Brute Force
longest-subarray-with-given-sum-k-positi.description: Enumerate every subarray and retain the longest one whose sum equals k.

longest-subarray-with-given-sum-k-positi.name: Brute Force
longest-subarray-with-given-sum-k-positi.description: Generate all subarrays, including those containing negative values, and track the longest target sum.

stock-span-problem.name: Brute Force
stock-span-problem.description: For every day, scan left until a greater price blocks the span.

sum-of-subarray-minimums.name: Brute Force
sum-of-subarray-minimums.description: Enumerate every subarray while maintaining its current minimum and accumulate the contribution.

next-smaller-element.name: Brute Force
next-smaller-element.description: For every position, scan right until the first strictly smaller value is found.

subarrays-with-k-different-integers.name: Brute Force
subarrays-with-k-different-integers.description: Enumerate every subarray and count it when its distinct-value set has exactly k members.

longest-substring-with-at-most-k-distinc.name: Brute Force
longest-substring-with-at-most-k-distinc.description: Start at every character and extend while the substring contains at most k distinct characters.

fruit-into-baskets.name: Brute Force
fruit-into-baskets.description: Try every starting tree and extend until a third fruit type appears.

number-of-substrings-containing-all-thre.name: Brute Force
number-of-substrings-containing-all-thre.description: Enumerate every substring and count it once a, b, and c have all appeared.

longest-repeating-character-replacement.name: Brute Force
longest-repeating-character-replacement.description: Enumerate every substring and compare its length with its most frequent character count.

max-consecutive-ones-iii.name: Brute Force
max-consecutive-ones-iii.description: Try every subarray and stop extending when its zero count exceeds k.

valid-parenthesis-string.name: Brute Force
valid-parenthesis-string.description: Recursively try each wildcard as an opening parenthesis, closing parenthesis, or empty character.

candy.name: Better
candy.description: Compute increasing requirements from both directions and take the larger requirement for each child.

length-of-loop-in-ll.name: Brute Force
length-of-loop-in-ll.description: Record the visit step of every node; the difference at the first repeated node is the cycle length.

single-number-i.name: Brute Force
single-number-i.description: Count every value in a hash table and return the one whose frequency is one.

single-number-ii.name: Better
single-number-ii.description: Sort the values so triplicates become adjacent, then locate the group with a single member.

single-number-iii.name: Better
single-number-iii.description: Use a frequency map and collect the two values that occur once.

climbing-stairs.name: Brute Force
climbing-stairs.description: Recursively branch into one-step and two-step moves from every stair.

climbing-stairs.name: Better (Memoization)
climbing-stairs.description: Cache the number of ways for each remaining stair so every state is solved once.

coin-change.name: Brute Force
coin-change.description: Explore taking the current denomination again or moving to a smaller denomination, retaining the fewest coins.

coin-change.name: Better (Memoization)
coin-change.description: Memoize denomination and remaining-amount states while preserving the unbounded take-or-skip recurrence.

partition-equal-subset-sum.name: Brute Force
partition-equal-subset-sum.description: Enumerate include-or-skip choices until a subset reaches half of the total sum.

partition-equal-subset-sum.name: Better (Memoization)
partition-equal-subset-sum.description: Cache each index and remaining half-sum state in the subset recurrence.

longest-increasing-subsequence.name: Brute Force
longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Try taking or skipping every value while carrying the index of the previously selected value.

longest-increasing-subsequence.name: Better (Quadratic DP)
longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Store the best increasing subsequence ending at every position and extend it from all earlier smaller values.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-ii.name: Brute Force
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-ii.description: At every day, branch between acting and waiting while tracking whether a share is currently held.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-ii.name: Better (Memoization)
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-ii.description: Cache the day and holding-state recurrence for unlimited transactions.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iii.name: Brute Force
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iii.description: Explore buy, sell, and wait decisions while tracking the remaining completed transactions.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iii.name: Better (Memoization)
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iii.description: Memoize the day, holding state, and remaining-transaction state.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iv.name: Brute Force
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iv.description: Explore buy, sell, and wait decisions while tracking the remaining completed transactions.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iv.name: Better (Memoization)
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-iv.description: Memoize the day, holding state, and remaining-transaction state.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-coo.name: Brute Force
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-coo.description: Branch over buying, selling, and waiting; a sale advances two days to enforce the cooldown.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-coo.name: Better (Memoization)
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-coo.description: Cache each day and holding state while retaining the two-day jump after a sale.

triangle.name: Brute Force
triangle.description: Recursively choose the down or down-right child on every row.

triangle.name: Better (Memoization)
triangle.description: Cache each row and column state in the downward path recurrence.

matrix-chain-multiplication.name: Brute Force
matrix-chain-multiplication.description: Try every possible final split for each matrix interval.

matrix-chain-multiplication.name: Better (Memoization)
matrix-chain-multiplication.description: Cache the minimum multiplication cost for every matrix interval.

maximum-sum-of-non-adjacent-elements.name: Brute Force
maximum-sum-of-non-adjacent-elements.description: At every house, recursively choose between skipping it and taking it while jumping over its neighbor.

maximum-sum-of-non-adjacent-elements.name: Better (Memoization)
maximum-sum-of-non-adjacent-elements.description: Cache the best non-adjacent total ending at each prefix.

house-robber.name: Brute Force
house-robber.description: Solve the two circular cases recursively: exclude the first house or exclude the last house.

house-robber.name: Better (Memoization)
house-robber.description: Memoize both linear ranges formed by excluding one endpoint of the circular street.

unique-paths-ii.name: Brute Force
unique-paths-ii.description: Recursively move down or right while rejecting blocked and out-of-range cells.

unique-paths-ii.name: Better (Memoization)
unique-paths-ii.description: Cache the number of obstacle-safe paths reaching every grid cell.

minimum-falling-path-sum.name: Brute Force
minimum-falling-path-sum.description: Start from every top-row cell and recursively try the three downward moves.

minimum-falling-path-sum.name: Better (Memoization)
minimum-falling-path-sum.description: Cache the best falling total from each matrix coordinate.

count-subsets-with-sum-k.name: Brute Force
count-subsets-with-sum-k.description: Enumerate take-or-skip choices and count leaves whose chosen values total the target.

count-subsets-with-sum-k.name: Better (Memoization)
count-subsets-with-sum-k.description: Memoize every index and remaining-sum count state.

distinct-subsequences.name: Brute Force
distinct-subsequences.description: Recursively skip each source character or match it to the current target character.

distinct-subsequences.name: Better (Memoization)
distinct-subsequences.description: Cache source-index and target-index states in the subsequence-count recurrence.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-tra.name: Brute Force
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-tra.description: Explore every buy, sell, and wait decision while charging the fee on each completed sale.

best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-tra.name: Better (Memoization)
best-time-to-buy-and-sell-stock-with-tra.description: Cache each day and holding state while applying the fee exactly once per sale.

minimum-cost-to-cut-the-stick.name: Brute Force
minimum-cost-to-cut-the-stick.description: Try every remaining cut as the first cut of each current stick interval.

minimum-cost-to-cut-the-stick.name: Better (Memoization)
minimum-cost-to-cut-the-stick.description: Cache the minimum cost for every interval between ordered cuts.

burst-balloons.name: Brute Force
burst-balloons.description: Choose every current balloon as the next burst, erase it, and recurse on the smaller sequence.

burst-balloons.name: Better (Memoization)
burst-balloons.description: Treat each balloon as the final burst in an interval and cache interval answers.

palindrome-partitioning-ii.name: Brute Force
palindrome-partitioning-ii.description: Try every palindromic prefix and recursively partition the remaining suffix.

palindrome-partitioning-ii.name: Better (Memoization)
palindrome-partitioning-ii.description: Memoize the minimum number of palindromic pieces for every suffix.

partition-array-for-maximum-sum.name: Brute Force
partition-array-for-maximum-sum.description: Try every legal next block length and recurse on the suffix after multiplying the block maximum by its length.

partition-array-for-maximum-sum.name: Better (Memoization)
partition-array-for-maximum-sum.description: Cache the best partition total obtainable from every starting index.

merge-intervals.name: Brute Force
merge-intervals.description: Repeatedly find and merge any overlapping pair until no pair changes.

leaders-in-an-array.name: Brute Force
leaders-in-an-array.description: For every value, scan its suffix and keep it only when no greater value exists to its right.

largest-subarray-with-0-sum.name: Brute Force
largest-subarray-with-0-sum.description: Start at every index, extend the subarray, and retain the longest range whose running sum is zero.

merge-two-sorted-arrays-without-extra-sp.name: Brute Force
merge-two-sorted-arrays-without-extra-sp.description: Copy both sorted arrays into one buffer, sort it, and split the values back into the original capacities.

maximum-depth-of-binary-tree.name: Better (Level-Order BFS)
maximum-depth-of-binary-tree.description: Count queue layers from the root until every tree node has been visited.

invert-binary-tree.name: Better (Iterative BFS)
invert-binary-tree.description: Use a queue to swap every node's children without recursion.

level-order-traversal.name: Better (Recursive Levels)
level-order-traversal.description: Collect nodes by depth with DFS, creating a result row the first time each depth is reached.

number-of-islands.name: Better (Breadth-First Search)
number-of-islands.description: Launch a queue traversal from every unvisited land cell and mark its full island.

clone-graph.name: Better (Breadth-First Copy)
clone-graph.description: Create cloned nodes lazily while a queue walks each original edge.

course-schedule.name: Better (DFS Cycle Detection)
course-schedule.description: Color prerequisite nodes during DFS and reject any edge that returns to an active recursion path.

dijkstra-s-algorithm.name: Better (Array Selection)
dijkstra-s-algorithm.description: Select the nearest unvisited vertex with a linear scan and relax its outgoing adjacency-matrix edges.

flood-fill.name: Better (Breadth-First Search)
flood-fill.description: Recolor the connected component iteratively with a queue.

bipartite-graph.name: Better (Recursive DFS Coloring)
bipartite-graph.description: Assign opposite colors recursively and fail when an edge connects equal colors.

topological-sort.name: Better (Kahn's BFS)
topological-sort.description: Repeatedly remove zero-indegree vertices to form a topological ordering.

undirected-cycle-detection.name: Better (Breadth-First Parent Tracking)
undirected-cycle-detection.description: Traverse each component with node-parent pairs and detect any visited neighbor other than the parent.

longest-substring-without-repeating-char.name: Brute Force
longest-substring-without-repeating-char.description: Start at every character and extend until the first repeated character.

longest-palindromic-substring.name: Brute Force
longest-palindromic-substring.description: Enumerate every substring and check it from both ends.

rotate-string.name: Brute Force
rotate-string.description: Construct every left rotation and compare it with the target string.

sort-characters-by-frequency.name: Better (Comparison Sort)
sort-characters-by-frequency.description: Count characters, then sort distinct character-frequency pairs before expanding the answer.

longest-common-prefix.name: Better (Sort First and Last)
longest-common-prefix.description: Sort the strings; only the lexicographically first and last strings need to be compared.

string-pattern-matching-rabin-karp.name: Brute Force
string-pattern-matching-rabin-karp.description: Align the pattern at every valid text position and compare characters directly.

valid-parentheses.name: Brute Force
valid-parentheses.description: Repeatedly erase adjacent bracket pairs until no more pairs can be removed.

next-greater-element-i.name: Brute Force
next-greater-element-i.description: For each query value, scan rightward in the reference array for the first greater value.

largest-rectangle-in-histogram.name: Brute Force
largest-rectangle-in-histogram.description: Treat every bar as a starting point, extend rightward, and track the minimum height in each interval.

kth-largest-element-in-an-array.name: Brute Force
kth-largest-element-in-an-array.description: Sort the complete array in descending order and select the requested rank.

top-k-frequent-elements.name: Better (Full Frequency Sort)
top-k-frequent-elements.description: Count all values, sort every value-frequency pair, and take the first k.

merge-k-sorted-lists.name: Brute Force
merge-k-sorted-lists.description: Append every list into one buffer and sort the combined values.

jump-game.name: Better (Quadratic DP)
jump-game.description: Mark every index reachable from an earlier reachable index whose jump covers it.

gas-station.name: Brute Force
gas-station.description: Simulate a complete circuit from every possible starting station.

jump-game-ii.name: Better (Quadratic DP)
jump-game-ii.description: Relax the minimum jump count from every reachable position to all positions inside its jump range.

check-if-a-number-is-odd-or-not.name: Brute Force
check-if-a-number-is-odd-or-not.description: Use the arithmetic remainder after division by two.

check-if-i-th-bit-is-set-or-not.name: Brute Force
check-if-i-th-bit-is-set-or-not.description: Repeatedly divide by two until the requested binary digit becomes the least significant digit.

check-if-a-number-is-power-of-2-or-not.name: Brute Force
check-if-a-number-is-power-of-2-or-not.description: Repeatedly divide an even positive number by two and check whether the process ends at one.

count-the-number-of-set-bits.name: Brute Force
count-the-number-of-set-bits.description: Inspect every binary digit by shifting the number one position at a time.

find-the-number-that-appears-once-and-th.name: Brute Force
find-the-number-that-appears-once-and-th.description: Count every value in a hash table and return the value with frequency one.

rearrange-array-elements-by-sign.name: Better (Stable Buckets)
rearrange-array-elements-by-sign.description: Collect positive and negative values independently, then interleave them while preserving the original order inside each sign group.

print-the-matrix-in-spiral-manner.name: Brute Force (Visited Simulation)
print-the-matrix-in-spiral-manner.description: Walk in the current direction and rotate whenever the next cell is outside the matrix or already visited.

pascal-s-triangle.name: Better (Direct Binomial Coefficients)
pascal-s-triangle.description: Build each row from the multiplicative binomial-coefficient recurrence instead of referencing the previous row.

lowest-common-ancestor-of-a-binary-searc.name: Better (Root Paths)
lowest-common-ancestor-of-a-binary-searc.description: Record the search path from the root to each key, then return the last shared node.

iterative-inorder.name: Better (Recursive Inorder)
iterative-inorder.description: Use the call stack to visit left subtree, node, and right subtree in inorder sequence.

middle-of-a-linkedlist.name: Brute Force (Length Counting)
middle-of-a-linkedlist.description: Count all nodes, then walk half the length to reach the middle node.

sort-a-ll-of-0-s-1-s-and-2-s.name: Better (Counting Rewrite)
sort-a-ll-of-0-s-1-s-and-2-s.description: Count zeroes, ones, and twos, then overwrite node values in sorted order.

add-1-to-a-number-represented-by-ll.name: Better (Digit Buffer)
add-1-to-a-number-represented-by-ll.description: Store node addresses, propagate a carry from the tail, and add a new leading node only if necessary.

palindrome-number.name: Brute Force (String Mirror)
palindrome-number.description: Convert the number to text and compare symmetric characters from both ends.

pow-x-n.name: Brute Force (Repeated Multiplication)
pow-x-n.description: Multiply the base once per exponent unit and invert the result for a negative exponent.

reverse-a-number.name: Better (String Reversal)
reverse-a-number.description: Reverse the decimal digits as text and restore the original sign.

isomorphic-strings.name: Better (Last-Seen Arrays)
isomorphic-strings.description: Compare the previous positions of corresponding characters, which simultaneously enforces both mapping directions.

roman-number-to-integer.name: Better (Expanded Pair Table)
roman-number-to-integer.description: Match the six subtractive pairs first; otherwise consume a single Roman symbol.

generate-parentheses.name: Brute Force (Generate and Validate)
generate-parentheses.description: Generate every length-2N parenthesis string and retain only strings whose running balance never becomes negative and ends at zero.

subsets.name: Better (Bitmask Enumeration)
subsets.description: Treat each bit of a mask as the include-or-exclude decision for one array element.

fibonacci-number.name: Brute Force (Recursive Definition)
fibonacci-number.description: Evaluate the mathematical recurrence directly to expose its overlapping subproblems.

validate-bst.name: Better (Inorder Materialization)
validate-bst.description: Store the inorder traversal and verify that it is strictly increasing.

kth-smallest-element-in-a-bst.name: Better (Iterative Inorder Stack)
kth-smallest-element-in-a-bst.description: Simulate inorder traversal with an explicit stack and stop when the K-th node is popped.

assign-cookies.name: Brute Force (Backtracking Matching)
assign-cookies.description: Try every unused cookie for each child and maximize the number of satisfied children.

maximum-xor-of-two-numbers-in-an-array.name: Brute Force (All Pairs)
maximum-xor-of-two-numbers-in-an-array.description: Evaluate XOR for every unordered pair and retain the maximum.

merge-sort.name: Better (Bottom-Up Iterative Merge Sort)
merge-sort.description: Merge runs of width 1, 2, 4, and so on, avoiding recursion while retaining stable O(N log N) merging.

bubble-sort.name: Brute Force (Classic Passes)
bubble-sort.description: Run all adjacent-comparison passes without early termination to show the direct baseline.

min-stack.name: Better (Value-Minimum Pairs)
min-stack.description: Store the minimum-so-far beside every pushed value so all operations remain constant time.

implement-queue-using-stacks.name: Better (Costly Push)
implement-queue-using-stacks.description: Move existing elements during push so the oldest element is always on top of the primary stack.

lru-cache.name: Brute Force (Recency Timestamps)
lru-cache.description: Keep values and last-used timestamps in a map, scanning for the least-recently-used key only when eviction is required.

01-matrix-bipartite-graph.name: Brute Force (BFS From Every Cell)
01-matrix-bipartite-graph.description: For each non-zero cell, run a fresh breadth-first search until the nearest zero is reached.

find-median-from-data-stream.name: Brute Force (Sorted Insertion)
find-median-from-data-stream.description: Insert each value at its sorted position, then read the center element or center pair.

largest-element-in-an-array.name: Brute Force (Sort and Select)
largest-element-in-an-array.description: Sort a copy of the array and select its final element.

maximum-consecutive-ones.name: Brute Force (All Starts)
maximum-consecutive-ones.description: Start at every index and count how long the run of ones continues.

find-the-number-that-appears-once.name: Brute Force (Frequency Map)
find-the-number-that-appears-once.description: Count every value and return the one with frequency one.

find-out-how-many-times-array-has-been-r.name: Brute Force (Minimum Scan)
find-out-how-many-times-array-has-been-r.description: The index of the smallest value equals the rotation count, so scan all positions.

find-square-root-of-a-number-in-log-n.name: Brute Force (Linear Candidate Scan)
find-square-root-of-a-number-in-log-n.description: Increase the candidate until the next square would exceed the input.

split-array-largest-sum.name: Better (Partition Dynamic Programming)
split-array-largest-sum.description: Try every previous cut and minimize the largest segment sum for each prefix and partition count.

painter-s-partition-problem.name: Better (Partition Dynamic Programming)
painter-s-partition-problem.description: Compute the best maximum workload for every prefix and number of painters by trying each last cut.

k-th-element-of-two-sorted-arrays.name: Brute Force (Partial Merge)
k-th-element-of-two-sorted-arrays.description: Merge the two sorted inputs only until the K-th element is produced.

count-number-of-substrings.name: Brute Force (All Substrings)
count-number-of-substrings.description: Extend every start position, track distinct characters, and count windows containing exactly K distinct values.

sum-of-beauty-of-all-substrings.name: Brute Force (Recount Every Window)
sum-of-beauty-of-all-substrings.description: Enumerate every substring and rescan its frequency table to compute maximum minus minimum positive frequency.

delete-the-middle-node-of-ll.name: Brute Force (Length Counting)
delete-the-middle-node-of-ll.description: Count the nodes, then stop at the predecessor of the middle node and unlink it.

sort-ll.name: Brute Force (Array Buffer)
sort-ll.description: Copy node values into an array, sort them, and write them back into the list.

print-all-subsequences.name: Better (Bitmask Enumeration)
print-all-subsequences.description: Use every bitmask from zero through 2^N minus one as a complete include-or-skip decision set.

count-all-subsequences-with-sum-k.name: Better (Subset-Sum DP)
count-all-subsequences-with-sum-k.description: Accumulate how many subsequences produce every reachable sum after each input value.

check-if-there-exists-a-subsequence-with.name: Better (Boolean Subset DP)
check-if-there-exists-a-subsequence-with.description: Carry a boolean table of reachable sums and update it backwards for each value.

check-for-balanced-parentheses.name: Brute Force (Repeated Pair Removal)
check-for-balanced-parentheses.description: Repeatedly erase adjacent bracket pairs until no further reduction is possible.

asteroid-collision.name: Brute Force (Repeated Adjacent Resolution)
asteroid-collision.description: Repeatedly scan for one right-moving and left-moving adjacent collision, resolve it, and restart.

sum-of-subarray-ranges.name: Brute Force (All Subarrays)
sum-of-subarray-ranges.description: Extend every subarray while maintaining its minimum and maximum, then add their difference.

remove-k-digits.name: Brute Force (Choose Remaining Digits)
remove-k-digits.description: Enumerate which N-K digits remain, reject leading zeroes through normalization, and retain the smallest resulting number.

the-celebrity-problem.name: Brute Force (Candidate Verification)
the-celebrity-problem.description: Check every person against every other person until one satisfies both celebrity conditions.

count-number-of-nice-subarrays.name: Brute Force (All Windows)
count-number-of-nice-subarrays.description: Enumerate every subarray and count it when the number of odd elements equals K.

maximum-points-you-can-obtain-from-cards.name: Brute Force (Every Left-Right Split)
maximum-points-you-can-obtain-from-cards.description: Try taking X cards from the left and K-X from the right for every possible split.

kth-smallest-element-in-an-array.name: Brute Force (Full Sort)
kth-smallest-element-in-an-array.description: Sort the complete array and read the element at index K-1.

task-scheduler.name: Better (Priority-Queue Simulation)
task-scheduler.description: At each time unit, run the remaining task with highest frequency while temporarily cooling recently used tasks.

hand-of-straights.name: Brute Force (Ordered Multiset)
hand-of-straights.description: Repeatedly start from the smallest remaining card and erase one copy of every value required by that group.

insert-interval.name: Brute Force (Append, Sort, Merge)
insert-interval.description: Append the new interval, sort all intervals by start, and run the standard full merge pass.

non-overlapping-intervals.name: Better (Longest Compatible Subsequence DP)
non-overlapping-intervals.description: Find the largest non-overlapping subset with dynamic programming; every other interval must be removed.

check-if-two-trees-are-identical-or-not.name: Better (Iterative Pair BFS)
check-if-two-trees-are-identical-or-not.description: Traverse corresponding nodes together in a queue and reject the first structural or value mismatch.

zig-zag-traversal.name: Better (DFS by Depth)
zig-zag-traversal.description: Collect nodes by depth recursively and reverse the insertion direction on alternating levels.

bottom-view-of-binary-tree.name: Better (DFS with Depth Tracking)
bottom-view-of-binary-tree.description: For each horizontal distance, retain the deepest node encountered during depth-first traversal.

symmetric-binary-tree.name: Better (Iterative Mirror Queue)
symmetric-binary-tree.description: Compare mirrored node pairs breadth-first, enqueuing outer children together and inner children together.

count-total-nodes-in-a-complete-binary-t.name: Brute Force (Breadth-First Count)
count-total-nodes-in-a-complete-binary-t.description: Visit every node with a queue and increment the count once per removal.

serialize-and-deserialize-binary-tree.name: Better (Preorder with Null Markers)
serialize-and-deserialize-binary-tree.description: Serialize a preorder stream containing explicit null markers and rebuild it using the same recursive order.

top-view-of-binary-tree.name: Better (Depth-First Column Map)
top-view-of-binary-tree.description: Traverse with column and depth coordinates, retaining the shallowest node seen in each vertical column.

morris-preorder-traversal.name: Better (Explicit Stack Preorder)
morris-preorder-traversal.description: Use a stack and push right before left so the next popped node follows preorder.

print-longest-common-subsequence.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Subsequences)
print-longest-common-subsequence.description: Enumerate every subsequence of the shorter string and retain the longest one that is also a subsequence of the other string.

reverse-nodes-in-k-group.name: Better (Stack per Group)
reverse-nodes-in-k-group.description: Collect each full group in a stack, append it in reverse order, and leave a final incomplete group unchanged.

flattening-of-ll.name: Better (Min-Heap K-Way Merge)
flattening-of-ll.description: Push the head of every vertical sorted chain into a min-heap and repeatedly append the smallest node.

bridges-in-graph-tarjan-s-algorithm.name: Brute Force (Remove Every Edge)
bridges-in-graph-tarjan-s-algorithm.description: Remove each edge in turn and test whether its endpoints remain connected.

articulation-point.name: Brute Force (Remove Every Vertex)
articulation-point.description: Temporarily exclude each vertex and compare the number of remaining connected components with the original graph.

armstrong-numbers.name: Better (String Digit Extraction)
armstrong-numbers.description: Use the decimal representation to obtain both the digit count and each digit before summing powers.

remove-outermost-parenthesis.name: Better (Explicit Stack Depth)
remove-outermost-parenthesis.description: Use stack depth before push and after pop to omit exactly the first opening and final closing bracket of each primitive.

largest-odd-number-in-a-string.name: Brute Force (Test Every Prefix)
largest-odd-number-in-a-string.description: Inspect every prefix, retaining the longest one whose final digit is odd.

maximum-nesting-depth-of-parentheses.name: Better (Stack Depth)
maximum-nesting-depth-of-parentheses.description: Push every opening parenthesis, pop every closing one, and track the maximum stack size.

word-break.name: Brute Force (Plain Recursion)
word-break.description: Try every dictionary word as the next prefix without caching repeated suffixes.

delete-node-in-a-bst.name: Brute Force (Rebuild Without Key)
delete-node-in-a-bst.description: Collect all values except the deleted key and insert them into a fresh BST.

ceil-in-a-bst.name: Brute Force (Full Inorder Scan)
ceil-in-a-bst.description: Traverse every BST value and retain the smallest value not less than the target.

floor-in-a-bst.name: Brute Force (Full Inorder Scan)
floor-in-a-bst.description: Traverse every BST value and retain the greatest value not exceeding the target.

recover-bst.name: Brute Force (Sort Inorder Values)
recover-bst.description: Collect inorder node pointers and their values, sort the values, then write the sorted order back.

largest-bst-in-binary-tree.name: Brute Force (Validate Every Subtree)
largest-bst-in-binary-tree.description: Treat every node as a candidate root, validate its subtree against bounds, and count it when valid.

n-meetings-in-one-room.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Subsets)
n-meetings-in-one-room.description: Enumerate every meeting subset, sort it by finish time, and keep the largest conflict-free selection.

job-sequencing-problem.name: Better (Backward Slot Scan)
job-sequencing-problem.description: Sort jobs by profit and scan backward for the latest free slot before each deadline.

surrounded-regions.name: Better (Disjoint Set Boundary Component)
surrounded-regions.description: Union adjacent open cells and protect every open cell connected to a virtual boundary node.

number-of-enclaves.name: Brute Force (Inspect Every Land Component)
number-of-enclaves.description: Explore each unvisited land component, record whether it touches a boundary, and count its cells only when fully enclosed.

word-ladder-i.name: Brute Force (Explicit Word Graph)
word-ladder-i.description: Connect every pair of dictionary words that differ in one position, then run BFS on the constructed graph.

power-set.name: Better (Recursive Include-or-Skip)
power-set.description: Build every subset with one recursive exclusion branch and one inclusion branch per element.

reverse-every-word-in-a-string.name: Better (String Stream Tokens)
reverse-every-word-in-a-string.description: Tokenize words with a stream, reverse each token, and join them with single spaces.

segregate-even-and-odd-nodes-in-ll.name: Brute Force (Value Buffer)
segregate-even-and-odd-nodes-in-ll.description: Copy even values followed by odd values into a buffer, then rewrite the list nodes.

add-two-numbers-represented-by-ll.name: Better (Two Digit Stacks)
add-two-numbers-represented-by-ll.description: Push both forward-order lists into stacks and build the result from the least-significant digits toward the front.

reverse-ll-in-groups-of-size-k.name: Better (Stack per Full Group)
reverse-ll-in-groups-of-size-k.description: Use a stack to reverse each complete group while connecting a final short group without reversal.

subset-sum-ii.name: Brute Force (Bitmasks and Set Deduplication)
subset-sum-ii.description: Generate every subset by bitmask and store sorted subsets in an ordered set to remove duplicates.

combination-sum-iii.name: Brute Force (Nine-Bit Masks)
combination-sum-iii.description: Enumerate all subsets of digits one through nine and retain those with K digits and the requested sum.

letter-combinations-of-a-phone-number.name: Better (Iterative Cartesian Product)
letter-combinations-of-a-phone-number.description: Start with an empty prefix and append every letter for each digit to form the next prefix layer.

m-coloring-problem.name: Brute Force (All Color Assignments)
m-coloring-problem.description: Enumerate all M^V complete color assignments and validate every graph edge.

number-of-nges-to-the-right.name: Brute Force (Right-Side Scan)
number-of-nges-to-the-right.description: For each query index, scan all elements to its right and count those strictly greater.

maximal-rectangle.name: Brute Force (Every Rectangle)
maximal-rectangle.description: Enumerate top, bottom, left, and right boundaries and verify that every enclosed matrix cell is one.

minimum-window-subsequence.name: Brute Force (All Starting Points)
minimum-window-subsequence.description: From every source index, scan forward until the target becomes a subsequence and retain the shortest successful window.

check-if-an-array-is-a-min-heap.name: Better (Recursive Parent Validation)
check-if-an-array-is-a-min-heap.description: Recursively verify both child relationships for every internal node.

sort-k-sorted-array.name: Brute Force (Full Comparison Sort)
sort-k-sorted-array.description: Ignore the displacement promise and sort the complete array with a general comparison sort.

find-minimum-number-of-coins.name: Better (Unbounded Coin DP)
find-minimum-number-of-coins.description: Compute the minimum coins for every amount, supporting arbitrary coin systems where greedy choice is not guaranteed.

preorder-traversal.name: Better (Explicit Stack)
preorder-traversal.description: Pop a node, record it, then push right and left children so left is processed next.

postorder-traversal.name: Better (Two Explicit Stacks)
postorder-traversal.description: Build root-right-left order in one stack and reverse it through a second stack to obtain left-right-root.

vertical-order-traversal.name: Better (DFS Coordinates and Sorting)
vertical-order-traversal.description: Collect column, row, and value triples during DFS, then sort them by the required vertical-order keys.

root-to-node-path.name: Better (Parent Map Reconstruction)
root-to-node-path.description: Traverse once to record every parent, then walk from the target back to the root and reverse that chain.

lca-in-binary-tree.name: Brute Force (Two Root Paths)
lca-in-binary-tree.description: Find root-to-node paths independently and return their final common position.

maximum-width-of-a-binary-tree.name: Brute Force (Unnormalized Complete Indices)
maximum-width-of-a-binary-tree.description: Carry complete-tree indices across each level and compute last minus first plus one.

print-all-the-nodes-at-a-distance-of-k-i.name: Better (Parent Graph BFS)
print-all-the-nodes-at-a-distance-of-k-i.description: Record parent links, then BFS outward from the target through left, right, and parent edges for exactly K layers.

minimum-time-taken-to-burn-the-binary-tr.name: Better (Parent Graph Multi-Direction BFS)
minimum-time-taken-to-burn-the-binary-tr.description: Record parent links and spread fire one layer per minute through parent and child edges.

bellman-ford-algorithm.name: Better (Edge-Count Dynamic Programming)
bellman-ford-algorithm.description: Use a fresh distance layer on each pass so layer K represents shortest paths using at most K edges.

floyd-warshall-algorithm.name: Brute Force (Bellman-Ford From Every Source)
floyd-warshall-algorithm.description: Run a single-source negative-edge-capable relaxation from every vertex to obtain all-pairs distances.

kruskal-s-algorithm.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Spanning Edge Sets)
kruskal-s-algorithm.description: Enumerate every V-1 edge subset, reject cyclic or disconnected subsets, and retain the minimum total weight.

kosaraju-s-algorithm-strongly-connected-.name: Optimal 2 (Tarjan Low-Link DFS)
kosaraju-s-algorithm-strongly-connected-.description: Track discovery times, low links, and an active stack to emit each strongly connected component in one DFS pass.

factorial-of-n-numbers.name: Better (Iterative Product)
factorial-of-n-numbers.description: Multiply the integers from two through N without recursive call-stack overhead.

generate-all-binary-strings.name: Better (Bitmask Generation)
generate-all-binary-strings.description: Format every integer from zero through 2^N minus one as an N-bit binary string.

subset-sum-i.name: Better (Bitmask Enumeration)
subset-sum-i.description: Enumerate each subset mask, accumulate its selected values, and collect every resulting sum.

palindrome-partitioning.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Cut Masks)
palindrome-partitioning.description: Treat each gap as a cut-or-join bit and retain segmentations in which every produced piece is a palindrome.

quick-sort.name: Optimal 2 (Hoare Partition)
quick-sort.description: Partition with inward-moving pointers around a middle pivot, then recurse on the two resulting ranges.

insertion-sort.name: Better (Binary Insertion)
insertion-sort.description: Binary-search the insertion position in the sorted prefix, then shift the intervening block once.

lfu-cache.name: Brute Force (Scan on Eviction)
lfu-cache.description: Store value, frequency, and timestamp per key, scanning all keys for the least-frequent least-recent victim only when full.

minimize-max-distance-to-gas-station.name: Better (Greedy Max-Heap Splitting)
minimize-max-distance-to-gas-station.description: Repeatedly add a station to the interval whose current largest subsegment is greatest.

construct-binary-tree-from-inorder-and-p.name: Brute Force (Linear Root Lookup)
construct-binary-tree-from-inorder-and-p.description: Use the postorder tail as root and linearly find it in each inorder slice before recursing.

search-in-a-bst.name: Better (Recursive Search)
search-in-a-bst.description: Recurse into only the subtree whose value range can contain the target.

find-min-max-in-bst.name: Better (Recursive Extremes)
find-min-max-in-bst.description: Follow only left children for the minimum and only right children for the maximum using recursion.

insert-a-given-node-in-bst.name: Better (Iterative Descent)
insert-a-given-node-in-bst.description: Walk to the first missing child in the target direction and attach the new node there.

delete-a-node-in-bst.name: Brute Force (Rebuild Remaining Values)
delete-a-node-in-bst.description: Collect all values except the target, then insert them into a new BST.

check-if-a-tree-is-a-bst-or-not.name: Better (Inorder Monotonicity)
check-if-a-tree-is-a-bst-or-not.description: Traverse inorder and require every value to be strictly greater than the previously visited value.

lca-in-bst.name: Better (Two Search Paths)
lca-in-bst.description: Build the root-to-key path for each value and return their last common node.

connected-components.name: Better (Disjoint Set Union)
connected-components.description: Union every graph edge, then count distinct component representatives.

dfs.name: Better (Iterative Explicit Stack)
dfs.description: Replace recursion with a stack, marking nodes when pushed to prevent duplicate work.

number-of-provinces.name: Better (Disjoint Set Union)
number-of-provinces.description: Union every connected city pair from the adjacency matrix and count unique roots.

rotting-oranges.name: Brute Force (Repeated Grid Sweeps)
rotting-oranges.description: At every minute, scan the grid and rot fresh cells adjacent to cells that were rotten at the start of that minute.

01-matrix.name: Brute Force (BFS per Target Cell)
01-matrix.description: Run a fresh BFS from each cell until a cell containing one is reached.

kahn-s-algorithm.name: Better (DFS Topological Order)
kahn-s-algorithm.description: Use postorder DFS and reverse the finishing sequence to obtain a topological order.

course-schedule-i.name: Better (DFS Three-Color Cycle Detection)
course-schedule-i.description: Mark nodes unseen, active, or complete; an edge to an active node proves a prerequisite cycle.

course-schedule-ii.name: Better (DFS Finishing Order)
course-schedule-ii.description: Detect cycles with three colors while appending completed courses, then reverse the result.

alien-dictionary.name: Better (DFS Character Topology)
alien-dictionary.description: Build precedence edges from adjacent words and topologically order the character graph with cycle-aware DFS.

shortest-path-in-directed-acyclic-graph.name: Brute Force (Repeated Edge Relaxation)
shortest-path-in-directed-acyclic-graph.description: Use Bellman-Ford style passes without relying on the DAG topological order.

shortest-path-in-undirected-graph-with-u.name: Better (Dijkstra Priority Queue)
shortest-path-in-undirected-graph-with-u.description: Apply general nonnegative shortest-path relaxation even though every edge has unit weight.

path-with-minimum-effort.name: Better (Binary Search plus Reachability)
path-with-minimum-effort.description: Binary-search the allowed edge effort and test grid reachability using only edges within that threshold.

cheapest-flights-within-k-stops.name: Better (Layered Bellman-Ford)
cheapest-flights-within-k-stops.description: Perform K+1 copied relaxation layers so each layer adds at most one flight edge.

network-delay-time.name: Better (Bellman-Ford Relaxation)
network-delay-time.description: Relax every directed signal edge up to N-1 times, then take the largest finite arrival time.

prim-s-algorithm.name: Better (Array-Key Selection)
prim-s-algorithm.description: Select the unused vertex with minimum key by linear scan and relax its incident edges.

number-of-operations-to-make-network-con.name: Better (DFS Components)
number-of-operations-to-make-network-con.description: After confirming enough cables exist, count connected components with DFS; components minus one cables must be moved.

most-stones-removed-with-same-row-or-col.name: Better (Explicit Stone Graph DFS)
most-stones-removed-with-same-row-or-col.description: Connect stones sharing a row or column and remove every stone except one representative per connected component.

accounts-merge.name: Better (Email Graph DFS)
accounts-merge.description: Connect consecutive emails belonging to the same account, then DFS each email component and sort its members.

number-of-islands-ii-online-queries.name: Brute Force (Recount After Every Query)
number-of-islands-ii-online-queries.description: Apply each land addition and run a complete flood-fill component count on the current grid.

swim-in-rising-water.name: Better (Binary Search plus Flood Fill)
swim-in-rising-water.description: Binary-search the water level and test whether cells no higher than that level connect start to finish.

cherry-pickup-ii-3d-dp.name: Brute Force (Two-Robot Recursion)
cherry-pickup-ii-3d-dp.description: Explore all nine pairs of next-column moves for both robots at every row without memoization.

partition-a-set-into-two-subsets-with-mi.name: Brute Force (All Subset Masks)
partition-a-set-into-two-subsets-with-mi.description: Enumerate one side of every partition and minimize the absolute difference from the complementary sum.

count-partitions-with-given-difference.name: Brute Force (Signed Assignment Recursion)
count-partitions-with-given-difference.description: Assign every value to the left or right subset and count leaves whose sum difference equals D.

longest-palindromic-subsequence.name: Better (LCS with Reversed String)
longest-palindromic-subsequence.description: The longest sequence common to the string and its reversal is the longest palindromic subsequence.

shortest-common-supersequence.name: Better (Merge Around an LCS)
shortest-common-supersequence.description: Reconstruct an LCS and append non-LCS characters from both strings around each common anchor.

print-longest-increasing-subsequence.name: Better (Quadratic DP Reconstruction)
print-longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Store the best LIS length ending at each index and a predecessor pointer for reconstruction.

largest-divisible-subset.name: Brute Force (All Subsets)
largest-divisible-subset.description: Enumerate every subset and retain the largest one whose sorted adjacent values divide each other.

longest-string-chain.name: Better (Hash Map by Deleted Predecessor)
longest-string-chain.description: Process words by length and derive every predecessor by deleting one character, looking up its best chain in a hash map.

longest-bitonic-subsequence.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Subsequences)
longest-bitonic-subsequence.description: Enumerate every subsequence and accept it when it strictly rises to one peak and then strictly falls.

number-of-longest-increasing-subsequence.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Subsequences)
number-of-longest-increasing-subsequence.description: Enumerate all subsequences, test strict increase, and count those whose length matches the maximum encountered.

longest-word-with-all-prefixes.name: Better (Hash-Set Prefix Checks)
longest-word-with-all-prefixes.description: Store all words and test every non-empty prefix of each candidate directly in the set.

maximum-xor-with-an-element-from-array.name: Brute Force (Filter Every Query)
maximum-xor-with-an-element-from-array.description: For each query, scan all array values within its limit and retain the largest XOR.

rat-in-a-maze.name: Brute Force (Queue of Complete Paths)
rat-in-a-maze.description: Carry a visited matrix and path string inside every queue state, exploring every simple route without recursive backtracking.

word-search.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Every Simple Board Path)
word-search.description: Generate every simple path up to the word length from every board cell and compare the produced string with the target.

combination-sum-i.name: Brute Force (Bounded Count Enumeration)
combination-sum-i.description: Enumerate how many copies of every candidate are used from zero through target divided by that candidate.

combination-sum-ii.name: Brute Force (Bitmask plus Set Deduplication)
combination-sum-ii.description: Enumerate every index subset, keep target-sum subsets, sort each one, and deduplicate them with a set.

sudoku-solver.name: Optimal 2 (Bitmask Backtracking)
sudoku-solver.description: Track used digits in row, column, and box bitmasks and branch on the empty cell with the fewest candidates.

implement-trie.name: Better (Hash-Map Children)
implement-trie.description: Store only existing outgoing characters in hash maps, trading predictable arrays for sparse memory.

implement-trie-ii.name: Better (Sparse Counted Trie)
implement-trie-ii.description: Use hash-map children with prefix and terminal counters to support duplicate insertion and erasure.

word-ladder-ii.name: Better (BFS Parent DAG plus DFS Reconstruction)
word-ladder-ii.description: BFS records every predecessor on a shortest layer, then DFS walks the parent DAG backward to emit all shortest sequences.

disjoint-set-union-by-rank-size.name: Brute Force (Quick Find Labels)
disjoint-set-union-by-rank-size.description: Store a component label for every element and relabel the entire old component during each union.

segment-tree-point-update-range-query.name: Better (Iterative Segment Tree)
segment-tree-point-update-range-query.description: Store leaves in the second half of an array, update ancestors upward, and query with two inward-moving indices.

fenwick-tree-binary-indexed-tree.name: Brute Force (Direct Array Range Sum)
fenwick-tree-binary-indexed-tree.description: Apply point updates directly and answer each range query by scanning its elements.

left-rotate-an-array-by-one-place.name: Better (Auxiliary Output Array)
left-rotate-an-array-by-one-place.description: Copy every element to its rotated destination, keeping the input untouched until the result is complete.

design-twitter.name: Brute Force (Global Tweet Scan)
design-twitter.description: Store all tweets chronologically and scan backward at feed time until ten followed-author tweets are found.

iterative-preorder.name: Better (Recursive Preorder)
iterative-preorder.description: Use the call stack to visit root, left subtree, and right subtree.

iterative-postorder-using-2-stacks.name: Better (Recursive Postorder)
iterative-postorder-using-2-stacks.description: Use recursion to visit both children before recording their parent.

iterative-postorder-using-1-stack.name: Better (Reverse Modified Preorder)
iterative-postorder-using-1-stack.description: Build root-right-left order with one stack, then reverse it to obtain left-right-root postorder.

preorder-inorder-postorder-in-a-single-t.name: Brute Force (Three Independent Traversals)
preorder-inorder-postorder-in-a-single-t.description: Run separate recursive preorder, inorder, and postorder traversals instead of sharing one stateful pass.

maximum-path-sum.name: Brute Force (Every Simple Node Pair)
maximum-path-sum.description: Convert the tree to an undirected graph and DFS from every node to evaluate every simple path sum.

check-for-children-sum-property.name: Better (Iterative Breadth-First Check)
check-for-children-sum-property.description: Visit every internal node with a queue and compare it to the sum of its existing children.

graph-representation-in-c-java.name: Better (Adjacency Matrix)
graph-representation-in-c-java.description: Store one matrix cell per possible edge, providing constant-time edge lookup at quadratic memory cost.

number-of-ways-to-arrive-at-destination.name: Brute Force (Enumerate Simple Paths)
number-of-ways-to-arrive-at-destination.description: Enumerate every simple source-to-destination path, track the minimum total weight, and count paths achieving it.
